verdict.answer_first: A is slightly more answer-first: it opens with “you must choose: keep answering... or refuse to answer,” while B starts clearly too but spends more time framing before the usable test.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits harder with “Ask one question” and a CP/AP table; B is decisive but more explanatory and caveat-heavy.
verdict.fabrication: A has an unsupported precise-ish claim: “99%+ of the time.” B has unsupported general claims like “partitions are rare,” but less numerically inflated.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because the table maps situations directly to “Choose CP/AP” and gives a concrete decision question.
